---
layout: ../../layouts/DocsLayout.astro
title: SEO
description: Title, description, Open Graph image, sitemap, and robots — what Opentree generates and how to override it.
---

The renderer auto-generates a reasonable set of meta tags from your `profile`. You only need to override fields when you want something different.

## What's auto-generated

For every published page:

- `<title>` — your `profile.name`
- `<meta name="description">` — your `profile.bio`
- Open Graph: `og:title`, `og:type`, `og:description`, `og:image` (your avatar), `og:url`
- Twitter Card: `twitter:card="summary"`, `twitter:title`, `twitter:description`, `twitter:image`
- JSON-LD `schema.org/Person` with `sameAs` populated from your link blocks (so search engines can map your social profiles to a single identity)
- `<html lang>` from `config.locale`

Plus, at build time:

- `robots.txt` (with a `Sitemap:` reference if `siteUrl` is set)
- `sitemap.xml` (when `siteUrl` is set)

All of this ships with every deploy and on local export.

## Overriding

To customize any of the meta fields, fill in the **Settings → SEO** section. The values land in the `seo` field of your config:

```json
{
  "seo": {
    "title": "Your Name — links",
    "description": "Custom one-line description for search snippets.",
    "ogImage": "assets/og.png"
  }
}
```

| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| `title`       | `string \| null` | Overrides `<title>` and `og:title`. |
| `description` | `string \| null` | Overrides `<meta description>` and `og:description`. |
| `ogImage`     | `string \| null` | Asset path or external URL. Overrides `og:image` and `twitter:image`. |

Any field set to `null` (or omitted) falls back to the auto-generated value.

## OG image best practices

- Recommended size: **1200×630**.
- File format: PNG or JPG.
- Keep important content centered — some platforms crop edges.
- Use a project asset (`"assets/og.png"`) if you want it shipped with the build, or an external URL if you host it elsewhere.

## Sitemap & robots

Both files are generated at build time and uploaded by every deploy provider (Vercel, Cloudflare Pages, GitHub Pages).

- `robots.txt` allows everything by default and points to the sitemap.
- `sitemap.xml` includes the root URL plus every `localeVariants` path.

To suppress them, leave `siteUrl` unset — without it, robots.txt has no Sitemap line and sitemap.xml is not emitted.

## Page locale

The `<html lang>` attribute is set from `config.locale`. See [Multi-locale](/docs/i18n) for the full story.
